manifest.title: Overall Composition Quality Across MRSA Variants
manifest.caption: Bar chart comparing the primary metric for all 10 model variants. This is the main results figure showing how the full Adaptive MRSA design compares against vanilla FreeCustom and targeted removals of similarity scaling, conflict routing, scheduling, and residual boosting components.
manifest.label: fig:fig_main_results_overall

manifest.title: Performance Under Matched vs. Mismatched Background Context
manifest.caption: Grouped bars compare each method under matched-background/shared-context and mismatched-background/clean-context evaluation slices where available. This figure tests whether Adaptive MRSA improves multi-concept composition robustly when contextual cues either align with or conflict with the target concepts.
manifest.label: fig:fig_context_split_results

manifest.title: Seed-to-Seed Stability of MRSA Variants
manifest.caption: Distribution plot of the primary metric across random seeds for the principal methods and key ablations. This figure assesses whether Adaptive MRSA improves not only average performance but also training/inference stability relative to vanilla and partially disabled variants.
manifest.label: fig:fig_seed_stability

manifest.title: Ablation of Similarity, Conflict Routing, Scheduling, and Residual Design
manifest.caption: Grouped ablation figure organizing variants by the component they alter: similarity estimation, conflict routing/deconfounding, phase scheduling, and residual boost design. It isolates which parts of MRSA contribute most strongly to composition quality and clarifies whether gains arise from the adaptive attention mechanism itself or from supporting training/aggregation choices.
manifest.label: fig:fig_component_ablation

markdown_refs:
![Figure 1: Bar chart comparing the primary metric for all 10 model variants. This is the main results figure showing how the full Adaptive MRSA design compares against vanilla FreeCustom and targeted removals of similarity scaling, conflict routing, scheduling, and residual boosting components.](charts/fig_main_results_overall.png)

![Figure 2: Grouped bars compare each method under matched-background/shared-context and mismatched-background/clean-context evaluation slices where available. This figure tests whether Adaptive MRSA improves multi-concept composition robustly when contextual cues either align with or conflict with the target concepts.](charts/fig_context_split_results.png)

![Figure 3: Distribution plot of the primary metric across random seeds for the principal methods and key ablations. This figure assesses whether Adaptive MRSA improves not only average performance but also training/inference stability relative to vanilla and partially disabled variants.](charts/fig_seed_stability.png)

![Figure 4: Grouped ablation figure organizing variants by the component they alter: similarity estimation, conflict routing/deconfounding, phase scheduling, and residual boost design. It isolates which parts of MRSA contribute most strongly to composition quality and clarifies whether gains arise from the adaptive attention mechanism itself or from supporting training/aggregation choices.](charts/fig_component_ablation.png)
figure_descriptions:
## AVAILABLE FIGURES (embed in the paper)
The following figures were generated from actual experiment data. Reference them in the appropriate paper sections using markdown image syntax: `![Caption](charts/filename.png)`

**Figure 1** (`charts/fig_main_results_overall.png`) — Overall Composition Quality Across MRSA Variants
  Caption: Bar chart comparing the primary metric for all 10 model variants. This is the main results figure showing how the full Adaptive MRSA design compares against vanilla FreeCustom and targeted removals of similarity scaling, conflict routing, scheduling, and residual boosting components.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 2** (`charts/fig_context_split_results.png`) — Performance Under Matched vs. Mismatched Background Context
  Caption: Grouped bars compare each method under matched-background/shared-context and mismatched-background/clean-context evaluation slices where available. This figure tests whether Adaptive MRSA improves multi-concept composition robustly when contextual cues either align with or conflict with the target concepts.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 3** (`charts/fig_seed_stability.png`) — Seed-to-Seed Stability of MRSA Variants
  Caption: Distribution plot of the primary metric across random seeds for the principal methods and key ablations. This figure assesses whether Adaptive MRSA improves not only average performance but also training/inference stability relative to vanilla and partially disabled variants.
  Place in: **Analysis** section

**Figure 4** (`charts/fig_component_ablation.png`) — Ablation of Similarity, Conflict Routing, Scheduling, and Residual Design
  Caption: Grouped ablation figure organizing variants by the component they alter: similarity estimation, conflict routing/deconfounding, phase scheduling, and residual boost design. It isolates which parts of MRSA contribute most strongly to composition quality and clarifies whether gains arise from the adaptive attention mechanism itself or from supporting training/aggregation choices.
  Place in: **Analysis** section


For each figure referenced, write a descriptive caption and discuss what the figure shows in 2-3 sentences.
